raw_text:
EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
October 2024 closed at $577,500 actual against a $680,000 budget, a net unfavourable variance of -$102,500 (-15.1%). The shortfall is driven almost entirely by a $82,000 Revenue miss that cascaded into a $6,000 Professional Services underbilling and a partially offsetting $12,000 Cost of Revenue underspend. The revenue miss comprises $70,000 in deal slippage across three accounts now expected to close in November and December, and $12,000 in permanent ARR loss to a competitor. The Professional Services shortfall is directly downstream of the same slipped deals, as SOW invoicing cannot proceed until primary contracts sign. Cost of Revenue underspent proportionally with lower-than-planned onboarding volumes. No cost overruns were identified in the period.

LINE COMMENTARY

Revenue | -$82,000 (-16.4%) | Budget: $500,000 | Actual: $418,000
The $82,000 shortfall reflects four deals that did not close in October. Three deals totalling $70,000 slipped to November or December due to procurement delays and one mid-cycle champion change, and should be treated as potentially recoverable:
- **Glenbrook Financial** ($45,000 ARR) — pushed to 15 November; procurement delay, signature reportedly pending.
- **Axis Media Group** ($28,000 ARR) — pushed to 1 December; new procurement lead now engaged.
- **Summit Retail Corp** ($9,000 ARR) — pushed to 30 November; however, a champion change triggered re-qualification, which introduces deal-outcome risk that requires confirmation before treating this as recovered.
The remaining $12,000 represents a permanent loss: Clearwater Tech was lost to competitor Apex AI on price. This portion should not be modelled as recoverable. No email-level confirmation of formal win/loss review was found; a formal Clearwater post-mortem may be warranted. The $70,000 slippage is expected to be absorbed into the November pipeline, though Summit's re-qualification status requires monitoring.
Sources
- CRM - 2024-11-07T26:15:00Z - crm-revenue-broad - crm-oct-pipeline: October pipeline close analysis. 3 deals pushed from October to November/December: Glenbrook Financial (ARR $45,000, close moved to Nov 15 — procurement delay), Axis Media Group (ARR $28,000, close moved to Dec 1 — procurement delay), Summit Retail Corp (ARR $9,000, close moved to Nov 30 — champion changed, deal re-qualified). One deal lost: Clearwater Tech ($12,000 ARR) to competitor Apex AI on price. Total miss: $82,000.
- Slack - 2024-11-07T12:15:00Z - slack-revenue-broad - sales-slack-2024-10-31: October close recap — we missed plan by $82K. Three slips and one loss. Glenbrook procurement just needs a signature. Axis new procurement lead is engaged. Summit is a re-qualify risk. Clearwater went to Apex on price — nothing more we could do. November plan needs to absorb the three slips.

Professional Services | -$6,000 (-12.0%) | Budget: $50,000 | Actual: $44,000
The $6,000 shortfall is directly downstream of the Revenue deal slippage. Two SOW renewals — Glenbrook ($3,500) and Axis ($2,500) — are contractually ready but cannot be invoiced until their respective primary software deals sign. A third new PS engagement is blocked pending completion of the Summit Retail onboarding, which in turn depends on the Summit deal closing. The value of the Summit-linked PS engagement was not confirmed in available evidence and requires clarification. Recovery of the Glenbrook and Axis SOW billings ($6,000 combined) is likely in November alongside those deal closings, subject to primary contract execution.
Sources
- CRM - 2024-11-21T26:15:00Z - crm-professional_services-broad - crm-ps-oct: Professional Services revenue from SOW projects. Two SOW renewals (Glenbrook $3,500, Axis $2,500) held pending primary deal close — will be booked when revenue deals sign. One new PS engagement delayed due to onboarding dependency on Summit close.
- Slack - 2024-11-21T12:15:00Z - slack-professional_services-broad - ps-slack-2024-10-31: PS miss is entirely downstream of software deals. Glenbrook and Axis PS SOWs are ready to execute but we can't invoice until the primary deals close. This will all normalise in November.

Cost of Revenue | -$12,000 (-15.0%) | Budget: $80,000 | Actual: $68,000
The $12,000 underspend reflects variable COGS scaling down with lower-than-planned new customer onboarding volumes. Two enterprise onboardings — Glenbrook Financial and Axis Media Group — were pushed into November alongside their respective deals, reducing hosting and support costs for the period. No Slack context was available for this line; the CRM explanation is directionally consistent with the revenue slippage and is noted as the likely driver, though the exact cost composition has not been independently confirmed. If both enterprise onboardings proceed in November as expected, associated COGS are likely to increase in the following period.
Sources
- CRM - 2024-11-15T26:15:00Z - crm-cost_of_revenue-broad - crm-cor-oct: Cost of Revenue analysis for October. $12,000 underspend reflects lower hosting and support costs from reduced new customer onboardings — two enterprise onboardings (Glenbrook, Axis) pushed to November with their deals. Variable COGS scales with revenue recognition.

INSIGNIFICANT VARIANCES
The following lines moved within acceptable tolerance and were not investigated further:
- **Software Licenses** — $3,000 unfavourable (-10.0%). Within normal range; no investigation conducted.
- **Support Contracts** — $500 favourable (+2.5%). Immaterial positive variance; no investigation conducted.
